channel.title: Rulebook on BotC - Lugdunum
channel.description: Recent content in Rulebook on BotC - Lugdunum

item.title: Setup
item.description:
You can set up a game of Clocktower by following the instructions on the setup sheet, there are instructions with lore.

Gather Your Players

Before secrets can be whispered and fate can begin its work, the gathering place must be prepared.

Arrange one seat for each soul who will take part in the story. The seats should face toward one another, forming a circle, square, or any shape that preserves a clear clockwise and counterclockwise order. Perfection is unnecessary, order is not.

item.title: Rules Explanation
item.description:
The rules sheet describes all the major things that a new player will need to know to start playing. Simply read out the text written on this sheet to the group, or let those who want to read it do so privately.

Part of the rules sheet will prompt you to talk about various hand signals. You’ll want to demonstrate these hand signals as you go, since some players learn visually.

item.title: Abilities
item.description:
Each character in Blood on the Clocktower has a unique ability as described on their character card, character sheet, and character almanac.

The character card give plain-language versions of the abilities that will serve you well in almost all cases, but if you run into a conflict or strange interaction between abilities and need to find a more specific explanation, follow the rules written in their character almanac

When used, abilities work immediately.

Example

If the Demon attacks the Fortune Teller, the Fortune Teller dies immediately. They do not get to wake up later that night to use their ability.

item.title: States
item.description:
In Blood on the Clocktower, players can be lots of things - this character or that one, drunk or sober, alive or dead, healthy or poisoned.

Of course, we don’t mean literally if Lankha is drunk or Paul appetites isn’t satisfied or Melho looks dead.

These are called states.

Basically, they’re permanent elements of a player that are independent from each other. For example, if you’re drunk and change your character, you stay drunk. If you’re poisoned, you can be drunk too.

item.title: Macchabées
item.description:
If your group has 5 or 6 players, you can play practically endless variations of Clocktower by playing Macchabées games (know as Teensyville in BOtC). The village of Macchabées lies just a few days’ walk from Lugdunum Bluff, and is perfect for player counts that are smaller than normal.

Each Macchabées script contains a different collection of characters. You can use the Script Tool to create them. All Macchabées scripts cater to 5 or 6 players by including only a few possible characters on the character sheet:

item.title: Script Tool
item.description:
Once you are familiar with the characters from the three scenario in the core set, you may want to create your own unique character lists. These are called scripts.

Do you have favorite characters that you like to put in nearly every game? Have you thought of character combinations that would make for interesting and challenging situations?

At https://script.bloodontheclocktower.com, you can create a script from any combination of characters you own. This tool will also generate a night sheet for a game with your custom character list, making it easy to run.
